en.extract:
The Bornean blue flycatcher (Cyornis superbus) is a species of bird in the family Muscicapidae. It is found in Brunei, Indonesia, and Malaysia, where it is endemic to the island of Borneo. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical moist montane forests.


References

BirdLife International 2011. [1].

The '''Bornean blue flycatcher''' ('''''Cyornis superbus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Muscicapidae]]. It is found in [[Brunei]], [[Indonesia]], and [[Malaysia]], where it is endemic to the island of [[Borneo]].<ref>{{cite book |title=Phillipps' Field Guide to the Birds of Borneo |author=Phillipps, Quentin |author2=  Phillipps, Karen |name-list-style= amp |year=2011 |publisher=John Beaufoy Publishing |location=Oxford, UK |isbn=978-1-906780-56-2 }}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s.
